title: Bias in Pretrained Models
concept: Because models are pretrained on raw web text, they absorb and amplify human biases, stereotypes, and toxic language present in the training corpora.

exercises.prompt: What kind of data are pretrained models typically trained on?
exercises.explain: Pretraining uses massive datasets of raw text from the internet, which includes blog posts, social media, and articles written by humans.

cards.title: Mirror of the Internet
cards.body: AI models seem super smart, but they have a hidden flaw: they copy our worst habits. Since they learn by reading the internet, they pick up our stereotypes and biases without even realizing it!

cards.title: What is AI Bias?
cards.body: Bias in AI happens when a pretrained model repeats and worsens human prejudices. Because these models learn from billions of words written by people on the internet, they accidentally memorize stereotypes, unfair generalizations, and toxic language.

cards.title: The Dirty Sponge
cards.body: Think of a pretrained model like a sponge soaked in soapy water from a dirty sink. When you squeeze the sponge, it doesn't just release clean water; it releases the dirty water it absorbed. Similarly, if a model drinks in internet text, it will squeeze out the unfair stereotypes and bad habits hidden inside that text.

cards.title: Stereotypes in Action
cards.body: If a model is asked to complete the sentence: 'The doctor called because...', it might write '...he wanted to discuss the charts.' But if asked about a nurse, it might write '...she was busy.' The model assumed doctor genders based on old stereotypes found in its training data.

cards.title: Myth: AI is Always Unbiased
cards.body: People think computers are perfectly neutral and objective. In reality, models are only as fair as the data they learn from. If the training data contains human biases, the AI will repeat them.

cards.title: The Data Pipeline
cards.body: Training data shapes how a model views the world. Clean data makes a fair model, while raw web text transfers human biases directly into the AI.

left.text: Contains stereotypes, toxic language, and unfair cultural biases.

right.text: Amplifies and repeats those same stereotypes in its answers.

exercises.prompt: Why do pretrained models often generate biased or stereotypical language?

options.text: They learn from raw web text which already contains human biases.
They are programmed by scientists to be intentionally mean.
They do not have enough computer memory to learn good words.
They prefer shorter words over longer words.

exercises.explain: Because internet text contains human prejudices, models trained on it will naturally learn and repeat those same unfair patterns.

exercises.prompt: Pretrained models are guaranteed to be completely unbiased because they are computers.
exercises.explain: Raw web text contains many human stereotypes and toxic language, which models absorb during the pretraining phase.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to show what models absorb from web text.
exercises.sentence: When trained on raw web text, a model absorbs human [blank] and stereotypes.
exercises.explain: Pretrained models absorb bias from their training data, which often consists of raw internet text filled with human stereotypes.

exercises.prompt: Identify which statement is generated by a biased AI model mimicking online stereotypes.

options.text: A software engineer designs computer programs using code.
The family cook must be a woman because men do not cook.
Cooking is a skill that anyone can learn with practice.

exercises.explain: AI models do not have personal feelings or opinions; they simply repeat patterns found in the text they were trained on.

exercises.prompt: If we train a large language model on millions of unedited public posts from the internet, what is the most likely outcome?

options.text: It automatically corrects human mistakes and makes the text fairer.
It memorizes and repeats the stereotypes and unfair views in the text.
It deletes all bad language automatically to protect users.
It ignores the meaning of words and only counts letters.

exercises.explain: Pretrained models act like mirrors of the data they are fed. If the internet text contains unfair assumptions or toxic language, the model learns and mimics those exact patterns.
